req.title: Message to provider (boundary)
req.statement: A patient shall send a message to their provider; messages containing attachments shall scan for disallowed PHI exfiltration patterns. Boundary cases on timestamps and numeric elements must be tested.

doc.reasoning: R-HLT-050 requires testing message delivery to a provider, PHI-exfiltration scanning on attachments, and boundary conditions on timestamps/numeric fields. Four tests are designed: (1) EP positive – plain-text message stored and provider notified; (2) NEG negative – attachment containing disallowed PHI pattern is blocked; (3) BVA boundary – message sent with timestamp at Unix epoch boundary (0) and a numeric field at its maximum allowed value; (4) STC state-transition – message moves through draft→sent→delivered states. Together these cover the happy path, error pathway, boundary values, and state lifecycle mandated by the requirement.

tests.name: test_send_plain_message_positive
preconditions: Patient is authenticated and session token is valid
Provider exists and is assigned to the patient
No pending messages in queue for this patient
actions: POST /messages with valid subject, body, and recipient provider ID
Assert provider notification event is recorded
expected: Provider notification stub records exactly one call
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_send_plain_message_positive(auth_session, provider_id, notification_spy):
    payload = {
        "to": provider_id,
        "subject": "Follow-up question",
        "body": "Is my next appointment still on Thursday?"
    }
    resp = auth_session.post("/messages", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    data = resp.json()
    assert "message_id" in data

    msg_id = data["message_id"]
    get_resp = auth_session.get(f"/messages/{msg_id}")
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200
    assert get_resp.json()["body"] == payload["body"]

    assert notification_spy.call_count(provider_id) == 1

tests.name: test_blocked_attachment_with_phi_pattern_negative
preconditions: Patient is authenticated and session token is valid
PHI exfiltration scanner is enabled on the /messages endpoint
A file containing a disallowed SSN pattern is prepared
actions: POST /messages with a multipart body containing an attachment whose content includes a raw SSN pattern (e.g. 123-45-6789)
expected: GET /messages returns no new record for this patient
trace: R-HLT-050: blocked when disallowed attachment detected
tests.executable:
import pytest
import io
import requests

def test_blocked_attachment_with_phi_pattern_negative(auth_session, provider_id):
    phi_content = b"Patient SSN: 123-45-6789 — confidential record"
    files = {"attachment": ("record.txt", io.BytesIO(phi_content), "text/plain")}
    data = {"to": provider_id, "subject": "Test", "body": "See attached"}

    resp = auth_session.post("/messages", data=data, files=files)
    assert resp.status_code in (403, 422)

    err = resp.json()
    assert err.get("error_code") == "PHI_EXFILTRATION_BLOCKED"
    assert "message_id" not in err

    list_resp = auth_session.get("/messages")
    ids = [m["message_id"] for m in list_resp.json().get("messages", [])]
    assert len(ids) == <pre_existing_count>

tests.name: test_timestamp_epoch_and_max_numeric_boundary
preconditions: Patient is authenticated and session token is valid
API accepts an optional client_timestamp field (Unix epoch integer)
API accepts an optional priority field (integer 1-9)
actions: POST /messages with client_timestamp=0 (Unix epoch lower boundary) and priority=9 (upper boundary)
Assert HTTP 400 response for negative timestamp
expected: Stored record has client_timestamp equal to 0
client_timestamp=-1 rejected with HTTP 400 and field-level error
trace: R-HLT-050: boundary cases on timestamps and numeric elements
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_timestamp_epoch_and_max_numeric_boundary(auth_session, provider_id):
    payload_valid = {
        "to": provider_id,
        "subject": "Boundary check",
        "body": "Epoch timestamp and max priority",
        "client_timestamp": 0,
        "priority": 9
    }
    resp = auth_session.post("/messages", json=payload_valid)
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    msg_id = resp.json()["message_id"]

    stored = auth_session.get(f"/messages/{msg_id}").json()
    assert stored["client_timestamp"] == 0
    assert stored["priority"] == 9

    payload_invalid = dict(payload_valid, client_timestamp=-1)
    bad_resp = auth_session.post("/messages", json=payload_invalid)
    assert bad_resp.status_code == 400
    errors = bad_resp.json().get("errors", [])
    assert any("client_timestamp" in str(e) for e in errors)

tests.name: test_message_state_transition_draft_to_delivered
preconditions: Patient is authenticated and session token is valid
Provider is online and reachable by notification service
Message state machine supports: draft -> sent -> delivered
actions: PATCH /messages/{id}/send, assert state transitions to sent
expected: PATCH /send returns status=sent with updated timestamp
After provider ACK, GET returns status=delivered
State never skips an intermediate state
trace: R-HLT-050: expected outcome – message lifecycle complete
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_message_state_transition_draft_to_delivered(
        auth_session, provider_id, notify_ack_fixture):
    payload = {
        "to": provider_id,
        "subject": "State test",
        "body": "Checking state machine",
        "status": "draft"
    }
    create_resp = auth_session.post("/messages", json=payload)
    assert create_resp.status_code == 201
    msg_id = create_resp.json()["message_id"]
    assert create_resp.json()["status"] == "draft"

    send_resp = auth_session.patch(f"/messages/{msg_id}/send")
    assert send_resp.status_code == 200
    assert send_resp.json()["status"] == "sent"
    assert send_resp.json()["sent_at"] is not None

    notify_ack_fixture.acknowledge(msg_id, provider_id)

    delivered = auth_session.get(f"/messages/{msg_id}").json()
    assert delivered["status"] == "delivered"
